tldr: Lucide is an open-source SVG icon library shipped as code packages, while Creatie is an AI-native product design tool. The realistic move is not a swap but using Lucide icons inside Creatie design files alongside Creatie's design and handoff workflow.
why_switch: You want a full design tool with AI-assisted UI generation, not just an icon set
You need prototyping, multiplayer editing, and developer handoff in one place
You are starting fresh and want AI-native workflows from day one
You prefer freemium pricing with public tiers over self-hosting
Your team needs design files, not just icon components in code
what_to_export: List of Lucide icons currently used in your codebase
Custom icon variants or modifications layered on Lucide
Icon usage map per component or screen
Color, stroke-width, and size conventions applied to icons
Naming conventions used in your design tokens
Existing design system documentation referencing Lucide
Any Figma or design file that mirrors current icon usage
export_method: Lucide is a code-first library, so there is nothing to export from Lucide itself; the artifacts you need live in your codebase and design files. Generate an inventory of every Lucide import across your repo (for example by grepping for 'lucide-react', 'lucide-vue-next', or similar package names) and capture the unique icon names. Save SVGs for each used icon directly from the official Lucide gallery on lucide.dev or from your installed package's SVG assets, keeping the same names. If you maintain a Figma or other design library that already mirrors your Lucide usage, export it as your source of truth for screens. Document stroke width, default size, and color tokens you currently apply so you can recreate the same look in Creatie. Treat this as a 'recreate, do not migrate' task.
import_method: Inside Creatie, create a project for your design system and a dedicated icon library file. Bring the SVGs in by drag-and-drop or paste, then convert each into a reusable component so screens reference one source. Apply your previous stroke and size conventions as component properties or styles where Creatie supports them. Rebuild key screens or components in Creatie referencing the new icon components. Use Creatie's developer handoff to inspect values, and continue using the Lucide npm packages in code if you want generated screens to map to icons that already exist in your bundle. Verify that icon names and visuals line up between Creatie components and code imports before opening handoff to engineers.

concept_mapping.target: Component style or property in Creatie

effort: 1-3 days for a small design system, 1-2 weeks if rebuilding many screens
gotchas: Lucide is an icon library and Creatie is a design tool, so this is a workflow change, not a like-for-like replacement
There is no automatic Lucide-to-Creatie import; expect to recreate components
Naming drift between code and design files breaks handoff if you rename icons during the move
Visual parity is easy to miss if you forget to set the original stroke width and size
If Creatie's plugin or library features differ from what you assumed, plan around manual updates instead
Lucide updates over time; pin a version so designs and code stay in sync

email.subject: Switching our icon and design workflow from Lucide to Creatie
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our design workflow into Creatie while continuing to use Lucide icons in code. Over the next 14 days we will inventory used icons, build a Creatie icon library, and rebuild key screens. Production code keeps Lucide imports for now. Please flag any custom icon variants you rely on so we capture them. We will share the new Creatie library and handoff link before cutover.

Thanks,
Your customer
